steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what topic both texts address. Text 1 argues the word "curry" is a colonial invention that should be abandoned. Text 2 describes a chef who uses the word "curry" despite knowing its colonial roots. Both texts center on the word "curry" itself — specifically whether and how people use it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The history about the peoples on the Indian subcontinent.': neither text is primarily about the peoples of the subcontinent — only the term applied to their food is discussed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The use of the term "curry."': Text 1 argues the term should be rejected; Text 2 shows someone who still uses it. Both engage directly with how the word is used. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The difference between madras and gato pima.': Text 1 mentions these dishes only to illustrate that "curry" is applied to very different foods; Text 2 never mentions them. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The best method for studying a region's cuisine.': neither text is about methodology for studying food. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
